title: Headless C++ physical-project index
summary: A standard-library-only Python process emits deterministic JSON for project files, inferred components, compilation profiles, direct include provenance, CMake File API targets, target consumers, component dependencies, and observed cycles; Neovim is a read-only client.
details: tools/cpp_project_index discovers an optional .dans-project.json, compile_commands.json, and an existing CMake File API codemodel reply without writing query or build artifacts.

Compilation commands are normalized by removing source, output, dependency-generation, compile-only, and PCH-injection arguments while preserving semantic flags, forced includes, and ordered include roots. Equivalent commands share a content-derived profile ID.
First-party C/C++/CUDA headers, sources, and module-interface extensions are grouped by logical stem across configured source/include roots. Explicit component overrides are supported for exceptions.

Every directly spelled include records its source line, delimiter, preprocessor branch stack, resolved provider, classification, search-root provenance, applicable profiles, shadowed alternatives, and provider component when known. Macro includes remain explicit unknowns.

Existing CMake File API replies contribute target type, sources, dependencies, artifacts, definitions, and include roots. Declared target ownership remains separate from downstream consumer targets propagated through includes.

Component include edges retain line-level evidence and distinguish interface from implementation origins. Tarjan strongly-connected-component analysis reports observed cycles without blessing or rewriting them.

lua/custom/cpp_tools/project.lua discovers roots, runs the process through latest-result-wins jobs, renders the component/dependency/diagnostic view, explains the include under the cursor, and can expose the raw JSON.
rationale: Header synchronization, include/build intelligence, object-flow analysis, ABI inspection, and a future module DAG all require the same stable project identity and provenance substrate.
A standalone deterministic protocol is testable without Neovim and isolates future compiler/CMake implementation changes from editor presentation.
Standard-library-only scanning keeps bootstrap cost low; semantic symbol provenance can later enrich the same versioned protocol through a native compiler sidecar.

provenance.note: Headless evidence engine and Neovim client.
Owner approved beginning with a headless shared project index before header synchronization and module code generation.

evidence.claim: Determinism, command normalization, cross-root component pairing, profiles, real-shaped CMake replies, ownership/consumption, include conditions, vendored providers, macro unknowns, edges, and cycles are pinned.
Root discovery, asynchronous indexing, summary rendering, cursor-line include explanation, conditional provenance, and source-byte preservation are exercised in headless Neovim.

questions: Where does header synchronization get project file and target ownership?
How can an agent inspect direct include edges and component cycles headlessly?
Does the C++ project scanner parse or configure CMake?
How are target ownership and target consumption distinguished?

last_verified.method: Synthetic project protocol tests, headless Neovim interaction, py_compile, and read-only dans-vk2 scan.
